LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR DEPOSITION PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF AYUMI NAKAMOTO IS HEREBY ORDERED TO APPEAR AND BE DEPOSED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION SHALL TAKE PLACE ON FEBRUARY 17 AND FEBRUARY 18, 2013 IN HONG KONG. THE DEPOSITION SHALL PROCEED FOR A TOTAL OF FOURTEEN HOURS (SEVEN HOURS PER DAY). A CERTIFIED CANTONESE LANGUAGE INTERPRETER SHALL BE PRESENT AT THE DEPOSITION AND PAID FOR BY DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PAY THEIR OWN TRAVEL EXPENSES TO AND FROM HONG KONG. THE COURT DOES NOT PLACE ANY OTHER LIMITATIONS OR RESTRICTIONS ON THE DEPOSITION. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT, IN ORDER TO ACCOMMODATE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO START THE DEPOSITION ON SUNDAY, FEBRUARY 17, 2014, PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY THE ADDITIONAL COSTS OF $1,400.00 FOR THE INTERPRETER AND COURT REPORTER AT THE TIME OF THE DEPOSITION ON SUNDAY OR OR BEFORE. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Deposition Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Continued To Feb-08-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Deposition Protective Order
Off Calendar Feb-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: WILLIAM TAYLOR, ESQ., 415.777.1688, FOR PLAINTIFF AYUMI NAKAMOTO.
WENDELL GODDARD, ESQ., 510.625.7777 AND ROBERT CHAN, ESQ., 415.459.8877, FOR DEFENDANTS KINFONG SIT AND YUZHEN XUE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR DEPOSITION PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF AYUMI NAKAMOTO IS HEREBY ORDERED TO APPEAR AND BE DEPOSED AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff's deposition shall take place on February 17 and February 18, 2013 in Hong Kong. The deposition shall proceed for a total of fourteen hours (seven hours per day). A certified Cantonese language interpreter shall be present at the deposition and paid for by Defendants. Plaintiff's counsel shall pay their own travel expenses to and from Hong Kong. The Court does not place any other limitations or restrictions on the deposition. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT, IN ORDER TO ACCOMMODATE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO START THE DEPOSITION ON SUNDAY, FEBRUARY 17, 2014, PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY THE ADDITIONAL COSTS OF $1,400.00 FOR THE INTERPRETER AND COURT REPORTER AT THE TIME OF THE DEPOSITION ON SUNDAY OR OR BEFORE. Request for attorneys fees denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Deposition Questions, And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM ZAK TAYLOR OF THE TSAO-WU, CHOW & YEE LLP, TEL. NO. 777-1688 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WENDELL H. GODDARD, TEL. NO. (510) 625-7777 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. ROBERT Y. P. CHAN, TEL. NO. 759-8877 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - DEFENDANT KINFONG SIT's Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Deposition Questions, And Production Of Documents IS DENIED AS PREMATURE BECAUSE UPON ITS FILING, THERE IS NO PENDING DATE OR PLACE FOR DEPOSITION. THE SANCTION IS LIKEWISE DENIED. THE COURT ENCOURAGED THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO A DATE AND TIME FOR THE DEPOSITION TO TAKE PLACE AND ALSO A MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER MAY BE FILED. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Mediation
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Y. P. Chan, Esq., 415.759.8877, and William Z. Taylor, Esq., 415.777.1688, for plaintiff Ayumi Nakamoto.
Wendell H. Goddard, Esq., 510.625.7777, for defendant Kinfong Sit.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT KINFONG SIT'S Motion For Order Compelling Mediation is denied. The Court has no authority to compel mediation and there was no agreement between the parties to mediate. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976
